contracts.input_contract: The declarative registry, unit-contract overrides, referenced scripts, and required artifact paths.
contracts.output_contract: Reject malformed, unordered, duplicate, stale-authority, missing-script, missing-artifact, or contract-incomplete registry state.
contracts.claim_scope: Validation orchestration metadata and filesystem coverage only.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Registry validity does not establish semantic adequacy of its validators or truth of validated artifacts.

contracts.input_contract: Pinned tested-build/deploy workflow tool versions, Lean toolchain, CITATION.cff, and release reproducibility ledger.
contracts.output_contract: Reject toolchain, citation, DOI, tag, commit, locale, or release-record drift from the v1.0 reproducibility contract.
contracts.claim_scope: Recorded build/citation reproducibility metadata; not actual cross-machine reproducibility.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Metadata consistency does not reproduce a build or approve a release artifact.

contracts.input_contract: Every GitHub Actions workflow, machine dependency-pin inventory/schema, reviewed action commits, Elan commit URL and digest, review dates, and prose ledger.
contracts.output_contract: Reject moving or unregistered remote actions, workflow/inventory mismatch, mutable or unchecked installers, stale or malformed pin reviews, and incomplete documentation.
output_assertions: Elan URL and digest match inventory
review due dates equal review date plus 90 days
contracts.claim_scope: Fetched CI dependency identity at declared workflow references.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Pins do not prove upstream code benign, runner integrity, or transitive dependency immutability.

contracts.input_contract: Tested Pages build workflow and workflow-run-triggered deployment workflow.
contracts.output_contract: Reject deploy-time rebuilding, wrong-run artifact authority, absent commit binding, or verification after Pages upload.
contracts.claim_scope: Static workflow authority and handoff structure.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Static workflow structure does not attest a GitHub run or public deployment.

contracts.input_contract: README and landing trust surfaces plus owning claim, evidence, reader, and contribution ledgers.
contracts.output_contract: Reject missing compact public boundaries, contradictory ledger-owned facts, or inflated core support counts.
output_assertions: dense transition details stay in owning ledger
contracts.claim_scope: First-minute public status and routing coherence.
contracts.prohibited_inference: A coherent trust surface does not prove the claims summarized there.

contracts.input_contract: Canonical status policy/schema, manifest, source inventory, claim dispositions, four active prose surfaces, and chapter headers.
contracts.output_contract: Reject stale generated blocks, contradictory active counts, global chapter-header counts, and when requested malformed local or deployed graphs.
output_assertions: one exact generated block per active surface
chapter headers carry no global counts
rendered/deployed chapter order and H1 shape match
contracts.claim_scope: Repository, rendered-site, or requested deployed-site status coherence.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Status agreement does not prove chapter claims, source completeness, or artifact quality.

contracts.input_contract: Versioned release policy, v1.0 release record, canonical current status, and optional rendered root/latest/index trees.
contracts.output_contract: Reject dishonest immutable artifact state, version/commit mismatch, changed latest mirror files, and malformed version index.
output_assertions: published immutable artifact requires URL and SHA-256
contracts.claim_scope: Release-channel identity and truthfulness.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Channel metadata does not create durable storage or prove a deployment occurred.

contracts.input_contract: Versioned release policy, deterministic archive builder, archive-candidate schema, tested-bundle format, and publication transaction documentation.
contracts.output_contract: Build and replay a clean synthetic tested-bundle archive and reject policy/commit mismatch, false publication, changed bytes, unsafe members, non-determinism, or source-bundle drift.
contracts.claim_scope: Deterministic packaging and identity of one tested-site archive candidate.
contracts.prohibited_inference: A deterministic local candidate is not publication, durable storage, independent review, runtime assurance, or evidence for chapter claims.

contracts.input_contract: Vector policy, all core-claim dispositions, generated 54-claim vector registry, and vector schema.
contracts.output_contract: Reject missing dimensions, invalid vocabulary, disposition mismatch, numeric aggregation, or automatic support effects.
contracts.claim_scope: Completeness and conservative semantics of evidence-quality metadata.
contracts.prohibited_inference: A vector records assessed gaps but does not independently validate quality or produce evidence.

contracts.input_contract: Reviewer-capacity registry and its schema.
contracts.output_contract: Reject false assignment, missing capacity sections, unmeasured availability, unsupported quality measurement, invalid substitutes, or false external-review state.
contracts.claim_scope: Oversight capacity record truthfulness, not human performance.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Valid role records do not establish competence, availability, independence, or review quality.

contracts.input_contract: Three specialist packet files, external-review program record, capacity roles, and scope artifacts.
contracts.output_contract: Reject missing tracks, assigned-state drift, missing packet sections/rubrics, false acceptance, support effect, or missing scope artifacts.
output_assertions: packets contain capacity, attacks, rubric, response record, non-claims
contracts.claim_scope: Review-program readiness only.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Packet readiness is not solicitation, independent review, evidence, or approval.

contracts.input_contract: Title and licensing decision records, author-decision schema, manifest/citation title, and operative LICENSE.md.
contracts.output_contract: Reject selected options while awaiting author, premature title drift, license grant signals, duplicate options, or support effects.
contracts.claim_scope: Decision preparation and current-state preservation.
contracts.prohibited_inference: A packet recommendation is not author authorization or legal advice.

contracts.input_contract: Author licensing decision, operative LICENSE.md, declarative provenance policy, complete Git candidate path set, generated inventory, schemas, and audit documentation.
contracts.output_contract: Regenerate path classifications and reject omissions, duplicate paths, premature clearance or license effect, external-source laundering, asset misrouting, decision drift, or operative-license drift.
output_assertions: every tracked or untracked nonignored candidate path classified
external source notes remain mixed-rights review
all current effects remain all rights reserved
contracts.claim_scope: Technical file routing and unresolved provenance state; no ownership or legal conclusion.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Complete path routing is not legal advice, author ownership assertion, contribution consent, license selection, or permission grant.

contracts.input_contract: Product contract/schema, release profiles, README, and landing product selectors.
contracts.output_contract: Reject missing or conflated narrative, reference, and registry audience/density/navigation/review boundaries.
output_assertions: correct release profiles and entry points
contracts.claim_scope: Product projection metadata and public routing.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Product separation does not produce standalone reviewed artifacts or prove content quality.

contracts.input_contract: Canonical manifest/status, product and contribution contracts, 15-chapter narrative spine, reader generator/overlays, projection schemas, and repository-backed evidence routes.
contracts.output_contract: Regenerate all three product routes and reject over-wide or out-of-order narrative selection, missing editorial jobs, omitted-reference loss, incomplete reference coverage, canonical-count drift, support promotion, or changed evidence snapshots.
output_assertions: 15 selected narrative chapters and 39 reference-routed omissions
contracts.claim_scope: Generated product navigation, bounded reader source selection, and registry snapshot identity.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Projection consistency and snapshot identity do not establish editorial approval, independent review, runtime behavior, or claim truth.

contracts.input_contract: Three-contribution contract/schema and active 54-chapter manifest.
contracts.output_contract: Reject more or fewer than three contributions, missing/duplicate chapter assignments, or independent chapter flagships.
contracts.claim_scope: Research-program scope control.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Assignment and focus do not prove novelty, importance, or empirical support.

contracts.input_contract: Governed repository-change schema, tracked scenario/attack/result/log bundle, runner, verifier, and documentation.
contracts.output_contract: Recompute matched baseline/governed results and reject missing attacks, false counts, unsafe governed releases, forged observation, rollback or residual inconsistency.
contracts.claim_scope: One disposable local Git repository-change workload under recorded scenarios.
contracts.prohibited_inference: The local workload does not establish production safety, general governance benefit, or transfer to AI deployment.

contracts.input_contract: Executed repository-change event log, invariant result/schema, Python derivation, Lean trace module, proof manifest/triage, and docs.
contracts.output_contract: Re-derive authority, revocation, evidence, residual, and causal-order checks and reject one mutation per invariant plus trace/proof-link drift.
contracts.claim_scope: Finite ordered events in the local governed repository-change log.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Finite log checks do not establish distributed clocks, complete observation, evidence validity, or deployed enforcement.

contracts.input_contract: Manifest-ordered outline proof-target tables and tracked proof manifest.
contracts.output_contract: Reject proof-manifest drift in tags, chapters, modules, statements, status, and order.
contracts.claim_scope: Proof-target metadata synchronization.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Manifest synchronization does not build Lean or prove theorem adequacy.

contracts.input_contract: All JSON Schema files under schemas/.
contracts.output_contract: Reject JSON parse errors and invalid JSON Schema declarations.
contracts.claim_scope: Schema declaration syntax and metaschema validity.
contracts.prohibited_inference: A valid schema does not validate instances, semantics, behavior, truth, or safety.

contracts.input_contract: README, contribution/license/citation files, issue templates, workflow, repository docs, and public/source metadata boundaries.
contracts.output_contract: Reject missing public repository surfaces, stale publication language, unsafe source metadata, or unsupported artifact/release claims.
output_assertions: public source inventory has no local private paths
contracts.claim_scope: Repository publication hygiene.
contracts.prohibited_inference: Publication hygiene does not approve content, claims, accessibility, licensing change, or release readiness.

non_claims: These exact contracts were internally audited and are not independent semantic review.
Exact artifact names and mutation controls do not prove validator completeness or correctness.
Un-overridden registry units retain class-level contracts until separately audited.
